headline: A genuine international DECIDE hub exists and is well-built — but underneath it is the domestic site with a fees toggle: course discovery, fees and entry are single dual-audience pages, there are ZERO country/region-specific journeys, and the agent / overseas-representative content is one page served under two URLs. Strong hub, generic depth.
what_exists: Two faces. (1) A purpose-built DECIDE hub /student-life/international-students (dir dd9bb0da4b975a32, 51 outbound links) curating the 'why Melbourne for international' story: forward links to /discover, /student-life/lifestyle, /student-life/cost-of-living, /student-life/settling-in ('Tips from international students'), /campus-and-facilities, /accommodation, /connect-with-us/view-our-brochures, plus 3 international meet-our-students profiles (Gautam/Jegath/Winda). International-House accommodation = exactly 6 pages (/accommodation/international-house + about/how-to-apply/alumni/faqs/scholarships). (2) The COURSE-DISCOVERY layer is dual-audience-on-one-URL: /find = 3,345 pages; 420 unique courses, 341 carrying a SINGLE /fees/ and SINGLE /entry-requirements/ tab serving BOTH cohorts inline — e.g. Master of Data Science /fees/ (c7b2fee4f6c64528) renders domestic AUD$44,992 first-year/$94,484 total AND international AUD$57,984/$121,767 on the same page, with <meta name='fees_international'> and <meta name='entry_requirements' content='IELTS 6.5...'> baked into the head. English/eligibility decide-content: /how-to-apply/english-language-requirements = 10 pages (UG + graduate + course-specific). The decide→apply tracks: /how-to-apply/{undergraduate-study,graduate-coursework-study,graduate-research,international-exchange-and-study-abroad}/international-applications, each with entry-requirements / applications / offers-and-enrolments / fees-and-payments (110 pages carry 'international' in the path). Agent channel: /how-to-apply/authorised-education-agent (36ece15a07d80710) + /how-to-apply/find-an-overseas-representative (368478b78b79f902).
friction: ELIGIBILITY/COMPLIANCE ACCURACY: international entry depends on per-course fee + IELTS figures that are dual-authored on a single domestic-shared page. The 341 /fees/ pages embed the international figure as <meta name='fees_international'> alongside the domestic one — if the toggle/markup desyncs, an international prospect can read a domestic fee or a domestic-cohort entry score and build a visa/financial plan on the wrong number (the GTE/financial-capacity test for subclass 500 keys off this).

STALE ELIGIBILITY DATA: English-language-requirements carries a still-published '2025-english-language-requirements' page AND _nocache duplicates (/2025-english-language-requirements, /2025-english-language-requirements/_nocache) crawled in 2026 — an out-of-date IELTS/band threshold at the decide stage directly feeds a wrong eligibility judgement before the applicant ever reaches the portal.

DEPRECATED-TREE HANDOFF AT THE DECIDE STAGE: the international decide-hub's 'Read visa information' CTA points to students.unimelb.edu.au/student-support/international-student-support/visas — the DEPRECATED legacy support tree (canonical is /support-and-wellbeing/international-student-support). A visa-curious prospect is sent into a deprecated, possibly diverging tree at the exact moment they are forming the visa picture. Same hub also deep-links /student-support/international-student-support/under-18-international-student-supervision-program and /visas/visas-for-your-family into the same deprecated fork.

NO LOCALISED RISK/COST CONTEXT: cost-of-living and lifestyle decide-pages mention 'international' but carry NO visa context (visa=false in body) and no country-specific cost framing — a prospect cannot see the visa financial-capacity / OSHC cost reality during the decide stage; that only surfaces later, on the wrong (logged-out current-student) side of the wall per the existing applicant-journey profile.

AGENT CHANNEL UNDERSPECIFIED: the single agent page tells applicants they 'can apply through an authorised education agent' but the public estate has no per-country authorised-agent list rendered (the agent directory is generic) — for markets where agents are the dominant channel, the decide-stage trust signal (is THIS agent authorised?) is not resolvable on-site.
fragmentation: ONE GENERIC LAYER, NOT LOCALISED JOURNEYS: ZERO country/region-specific landing pages exist (no /china, /india, /your-country pattern anywhere in 4,048 study.unimelb pages). The international audience gets a single global overlay; discover/decide content (cost-of-living, lifestyle, discover) is SHARED single pages, not internationally forked.

DUPLICATE-URL AGENT CONTENT: /authorised-education-agent and /find-an-overseas-representative are BYTE-IDENTICAL (both 9,100 chars of body text, identical hash) — one page published under two URLs. The 'thin (1 page) agent content' is really one asset double-counted, splitting inbound links and SEO signal across two addresses.

DUAL-AUDIENCE-ON-ONE-PAGE vs SEPARATE-TRACK SPLIT: international fees/entry are merged into the domestic course page (toggle model) at /find, but the application narrative is RE-AUTHORED per level (UG/GradCW/GradResearch each own a full international-applications subtree). So the same audience is sometimes a toggle and sometimes a separate tree — inconsistent mental model across the discover→apply boundary.

INBOUND EXCHANGE CO-MINGLED: /how-to-apply/international-exchange-and-study-abroad-applications (18 pages, its own tuition-fees/important-dates/getting-ready/returning-home) is a near-separate inbound-mobility PRODUCT living under the same /how-to-apply roof as the degree-seeking international journey — two different audiences, one shelf.

DECIDE CONTENT SPANS 4+ TOP-LEVEL SECTIONS: the international decide story is scattered across /student-life (lifestyle, cost-of-living, settling-in, meet-our-students), /discover, /accommodation/international-house, /how-to-apply (english, definitions, agent) and /connect-with-us (brochures) — assembled only by the curated hub's 51 hand-placed links, with no canonical 'International students' spine that owns the sequence.
the_seam: The decide→apply handoff into the walled portal. The international-applications 'Applications' page (e7c2dd25f76922c6) narrates eStudent/eApplication and routes via the bridge page /how-to-apply/your-online-application (70ed708019098089), whose only real exit is the uncrawlable T1 eStudent portal: https://prod.ss.unimelb.edu.au/student/SM/eApplications/eAppLogin.aspx (with a t1cloud.com eStudent mirror). That seam is bridged cleanly. The HIDDEN seam is the SIDEWAYS one: while still deciding, the international hub pushes the visa/under-18/family-visa questions OUT of the recruitment estate and INTO students.unimelb's DEPRECATED /student-support/international-student-support tree — so the prospect crosses from study.unimelb into a logged-out, deprecated current-student fork before they have applied, and (per the existing applicant-journey profile) the same OSHC/CoE steps then resolve to a different URL per audience track downstream. Decide also reaches into handbook.unimelb for authoritative per-course entry/English detail (study→handbook = thousands of links), a third system the decide stage silently depends on.
recommendation: Keep the strong /student-life/international-students decide-hub as the canonical 'International students' spine, but fix three depth gaps. (1) ACCURACY GUARDRAIL on the dual-audience course pages: treat fees_international + entry_requirements meta as a governed compliance field with an explicit 'last verified / year' stamp and an automated check that domestic and international figures cannot silently desync; retire the still-live 2025-english-language-requirements page and its _nocache duplicates so no stale IELTS threshold drives an eligibility judgement. (2) RE-POINT every decide-stage handoff (the hub's 'Read visa information', under-18, visas-for-your-family links) from the DEPRECATED /student-support/international-student-support/* tree to the canonical /support-and-wellbeing/international-student-support/* tree — a decide-stage prospect should never be seeded into a deprecated fork. (3) COLLAPSE the byte-identical /authorised-education-agent and /find-an-overseas-representative into one canonical URL with a 301 from the other, and add the visa financial-capacity / OSHC cost reality to the cost-of-living decide page so prospects see the true cost-of-decision before the portal — not after, on the wrong side of the enrolment wall. Net: the international audience is served distinctly at the hub, but below the hub it is the domestic estate with a fees toggle and no localisation; close the accuracy + deprecated-link gaps before adding country journeys.

headline: Listed-country applicants CAN self-serve eligibility from on-page tables — but everyone else falls into an unscoped "case-by-case" human funnel, and a stale "2025" requirements fork is still live and still linked from the current 2026 hub.
what_exists: study.unimelb /how-to-apply/english-language-requirements: spec count 10, but the 8 crawled URLs collapse to ~4 unique pages — a hub (ffb55c1f9159baed), an Undergraduate page (9d37416d259471e9), a Graduate page (8db0627eeaaffb74), and a year-stamped "2025 English language requirements" archive (46a726c7472d9294); the remainder are trailing-slash + /_nocache duplicates that inflate the count. The hub defines two routes: (a) "English-speaking background" exemption (citizen/10-yr resident of a "recognised English-speaking country" who completed secondary/tertiary education in English), and (b) accepted proficiency tests, linking out to 8 external provider sites (IELTS, IELTS One Skill Retake, TOEFL iBT, PTE Academic, Cambridge, LanguageCert, Michigan/MET). The UG page (2,500 words, 3 tables) carries a genuine per-country academic-equivalency table — UK GCE/Cambridge Pre-U/GCSE, Australia VCE, Canada (Alberta/BC/Ontario named individually), Germany, Hong Kong HKDSE, India CBSE/CISCE, Ireland, IB. The PG page (4,636 words, 10 tables) carries course-specific thresholds (6.5/7.0 dominate; "7.0" appears 89x) plus bridging pathways (UMELBP, Hawthorn-Melbourne). So for a LISTED country, eligibility IS self-serviceable on-page without contacting anyone.
friction: IMMIGRATION/COMPLIANCE: Non-standard or unlisted qualifications route to 'assessed on a case-by-case basis' (UG page) with NO transactional form, no SLA, no named assessor — it defaults to the generic site-wide 'Contact us'/Support-centre funnel (the only contact CTA on hub, UG and PG is the boilerplate 'How can we help?' block). An applicant whose country is not in the table cannot determine eligibility self-service; this gates everything downstream (offer -> CoE -> subclass-500 visa), and an applicant who guesses wrong wastes an intake cycle.

STALENESS/COMPLIANCE: A '2025 English language requirements' page (46a726c7472d9294, 3,441 words) is STILL LIVE and is STILL LINKED from the current hub (verified in links.json) as of today 2026-06-28. It mentions '2025' 6x and '2026' only 1x. An applicant can land on year-old thresholds and apply against superseded numbers — a high-stakes error when a 0.5-band miss means no offer and no visa.

EXEMPTION TRAP: The 'English-speaking background' rule reads as country-based but carries silent exclusions — e.g. Sri Lankan A-Levels are EXPLICITLY excluded from the exemption ('must satisfy ... by completing an approved English language proficiency test') and several Canada rows are gated to 'only for students completing the qualification in Canada'. These caveats are buried in table prose, easy to miss, and reversing a wrong assumption late is costly.

EXTERNAL-DEPENDENCY: 8 of the decision-critical links leave the estate to third-party test providers (ielts.org, ets.org/toefl, pearsonpte.com, cambridgeenglish.org, etc.). The University does not host its own at-a-glance comparison of equivalent scores across all accepted tests in one place; the applicant must reconcile thresholds across the PG page tables and each provider's own scale.

HANDOFF-INTO-WALLED-SYSTEM: Document submission points at 'eStudent', the 'application portal' and /how-to-apply/instructions-to-upload-documents, but the eApplications portal is walled/uncrawled — the public estate explains WHAT to upload but cannot show or validate the actual upload step where an eligibility error would surface.
fragmentation: Year-forking: a deprecated '2025-english-language-requirements' page coexists with the canonical pages AND is linked from the live hub — the same anti-pattern as the three forked current-student support trees, now appearing on the prospective side.

URL duplication: each unique page exists in trailing-slash and /_nocache variants (8 crawled -> ~4 real), so 'the 10 pages' overstates the genuine content footprint and dilutes link equity / analytics across near-identical URLs.

UG vs PG split is sensible, but the country-equivalency authority lives ONLY on the UG page; the PG page is threshold/test-score-centric, so a graduate applicant relying on a school qualification for the exemption must cross-read the UG table — the academic-equivalency 'source of truth' is not co-located with the audience that also needs it.

Stage-to-stage seam break: the post-arrival Diagnostic English Language Assessment (DELA, 613 inbound links on services.unimelb — a real placement/condition for some commencing students) is NOT referenced anywhere on the prospective English pages, so the 'English requirement' story silently restarts after arrival with no forward signposting.
the_seam: The decisive handoff is the "case-by-case basis" assessment for any qualification not in the on-page country table. For listed countries the page IS the decision; for everyone else the page hands off into an unscoped, formless human queue (the generic Support-centre/Contact funnel) and then into the WALLED eStudent eApplications portal (uncrawled) where documents are actually judged. The public estate guides the standard case well but provides no transactional path, SLA, or eligibility-checker for the edge case — and silently, the requirement re-emerges post-arrival as DELA, which the prospective pages never mention.
recommendation: 1) UNPUBLISH / 301 the live '2025 English language requirements' page and remove its link from the hub — never expose year-forked thresholds; keep a single dated 'current requirements' page (highest-priority fix, direct visa-error risk). 2) Publish the full list of recognised English-speaking countries and an expanded equivalency table (move/duplicate the UG country table to a shared component visible from BOTH UG and PG), and add a lightweight 'check your country' chooser so non-listed applicants get a definitive 'you must test' answer without contacting anyone. 3) Replace the generic 'Contact us' fallback for case-by-case assessments with a named, SLA-bearing intake (form -> admissions queue) so edge-case eligibility is traceable. 4) Collapse the /_nocache and trailing-slash duplicates to one canonical URL each. 5) Add a one-line forward signpost from the prospective English pages to post-arrival DELA so the requirement story is continuous across the lifecycle. 6) Provide a single on-site cross-test score-equivalence table so applicants are not forced to reconcile 8 external provider scales themselves.

headline: The agent channel that most international students actually use to apply is content-rich but functionally undiscoverable and duplicated across two URLs; the application itself silently forks across three systems, and the eStudent eApplications launch never appears in the public link graph at all.
what_exists: Not the '1 thin page' the brief assumed: a ~27-page /how-to-apply international cluster on study.unimelb (crawled 24 Jun 2026), with fractured authority. AGENT CONTENT: /how-to-apply/authorised-education-agent (hash 36ece15a07d80710, 9,102 chars of real content) is substantive: sectioned nav (About / Who must apply / Find an agent / nomination form / FAQ), a live external agent directory (eap.ascentone.com/unimelb, linked 4x), a downloadable nomination/authorisation PDF (UM_EdAgentNomAuthorisationForm.pdf), fraud guidance ('Only the authorised education agents listed are trained, approved and permitted... genuinely authorised'), scope limits ('cannot influence selection decisions, guarantee admission, or waive entry requirements'), and CoE/offer scope ('assisting you in accepting an offer... including through the provision of the conditional CoE process'). PORTAL-SEAM SHELL: /how-to-apply/your-online-application (hash 70ed708019098089, 264KB) + /your-online-application/application-help ('Application portal help') + /instructions-to-upload-documents + /fees/application-fee + /indicative-turn-around-times + /conditional-confirmation-of-enrolment. DEFINITIONS GATE: /domestic-and-international-student-definitions (hash b0d344d257df33b8) cleanly defines the domestic/international line (citizenship, PR, temporary resident, dual citizenship) that determines the entire downstream visa journey. study.unimelb is the heaviest estate (median 304KB; agent page ~245KB), slowest to load for the offshore/mobile audience that depends on it most.
friction: [COMPLIANCE/IMMIGRATION] The MANDATORY-agent rule is buried and never reaches the people it binds. The agent page states verbatim: 'Pakistan: All graduate degree applicants', 'India: Any international applicant who completed tertiary studies in India', 'China: All applicants who last studied in China can either apply through an authorised education agent or share their academic transcripts using the CSSD'. For these cohorts the agent channel is not optional but the ONLY valid application path; getting it wrong means an invalid application, delayed or withdrawn offer, and a knock-on delay to the CoE and the subclass-500 student visa.

[DISCOVERABILITY] The agent page is functionally undiscoverable via in-text links: anchor-text.csv contains ZERO contextual anchors reading 'education agent' or 'overseas representative' anywhere in the 20-domain estate. It is reachable essentially only by drilling the /how-to-apply left-nav, so an applicant who does not already know to look will never be told the mandatory channel exists.

[PORTAL SEAM] The actual 'Apply now / log in to your application' launch into eStudent eApplications is JS-injected and absent from the crawlable link graph. A whole-estate scan of study.unimelb links.json surfaces no eStudent/eApplicant/prod.ss launch URL; the only application-system URL exposed is studentit.unimelb.edu.au/news/troubleshooting-application-estudent-portal (a troubleshooting article). The 'Apply now' anchors that DO exist in anchor-text.csv point to unrelated systems (mentoring.unimelb x7, ctrs.app.unimelb x4), not the application portal. The single most decisive click in the funnel is invisible to navigation analysis and unguided in the static estate.

[CATALOGUE BLIND SPOT] Per-course 'How to apply' pages (the dominant entry path, ~128 instances across ~2,944 catalogue pages) do not surface the agent rule. Sampled Doctor of Medicine /how-to-apply (hash 8a09ed3b385e7981) links to /your-online-application but contains NO link to /authorised-education-agent or /find-an-overseas-representative. An India/Pakistan/China applicant browsing by course is routed straight to the portal and never told they must apply through an agent.

[BROKEN ANCHORS] The agent page's own in-page section links are malformed with double-hash hrefs ('##navigation-find-an-agent', '##navigation-agent-form'), so the on-page jump links to 'Find an agent in your country' and the nomination/authorisation form do not resolve.

[LOAD/EQUITY] At ~245KB the agent page (and the 264KB portal page) is among the heaviest in the estate, served to the audience most likely on constrained offshore/mobile connections at the highest-stakes moment.
fragmentation: DUPLICATE URLs, one page: /how-to-apply/authorised-education-agent (36ece15a07d80710) and /how-to-apply/find-an-overseas-representative (368478b78b79f902) are byte-for-byte identical content (both 9,102 chars cleaned, identical text == True; meta byteLength 245,569 vs 245,575) under the SAME title 'Find an authorised education agent or representative'. This is why prior analysis counted '1 page' twice. Two URLs split link equity, dilute search authority, and create canonical ambiguity at the journey's most compliance-sensitive page.

THREE-WAY APPLICATION FORK (corroborated by prospective-experience-profiles.json): the apply action splits silently across VTAC (UG domestic), the walled eStudent eApplications portal (international + graduate), and a CRM enquiry form. study.unimelb explains the fork only via prose cross-links (e.g. /your-online-application links 'apply via VTAC' for UG domestic), with no single decision page that says 'which system applies to YOU'.

PORTAL HELP SCATTER: post-submit guidance is spread across at least four sibling pages (your-online-application, application-help, instructions-to-upload-documents, indicative-turn-around-times) plus an off-estate studentit troubleshooting article on a different subdomain, rather than consolidated at the point of the portal handoff.

TWO online-application URL variants crawled (70ed708019098089 root + 91d325dcd9a96c93 /application-help), and the conditional-CoE step lives as its own thin page (hash c2633aa692ce1721) outside the agent/portal cluster.
the_seam: The apply -> portal handoff. The public estate carries the applicant up to the threshold (agent directory at eap.ascentone.com/unimelb; document-upload, application-fee, turnaround, and conditional-CoE pages) and then hands off into the walled eStudent eApplications portal via a launch action that is NOT in the crawlable link graph (JS-injected; only a troubleshooting article is statically linked). A parallel external seam runs through the AscentOne agent directory and the CSSD/CHSI transcript service (chsi.com.cn). For the mandatory-channel cohorts (India, Pakistan, China) the seam is doubly fragile: the applicant must FIRST find an agent via a page nothing in-text links to, THEN that agent drives the eStudent submission and conditional-CoE acceptance on their behalf, so the University's public guidance goes dark exactly when the highest-immigration-risk transaction begins.
recommendation: 1) CANONICALISE the duplicate immediately: 301 /find-an-overseas-representative to /authorised-education-agent (or set rel=canonical) to consolidate authority on the single compliance-critical page. 2) SURFACE the mandatory-agent rule where applicants actually are: inject an 'If you studied in India / Pakistan / China you must apply through an authorised education agent' callout into every per-course /how-to-apply page and into /your-online-application BEFORE the portal launch, each deep-linking the agent directory. 3) MAKE THE PORTAL SEAM VISIBLE AND OWNED: add an explicit, statically-linked 'Start / log in to your application (eStudent)' CTA plus a one-screen 'which application system applies to you' decision page (VTAC vs eStudent vs agent vs enquiry) so the three-way fork is resolved before the handoff, not after. 4) FIX the malformed double-hash in-page anchors on the agent page. 5) ADD a short post-submit roadmap at the seam (offer -> deposit -> conditional then full CoE -> visa -> OSHC) so applicants understand what the portal triggers next. 6) WEIGHT BUDGET: trim the ~245-264KB page weight on these offshore-critical pages.

stage: Offer, CoE & the ESOS framework (international legal core: accept offer -> tuition deposit -> conditional vs full CoE -> CoE, the ESOS document without which there is no subclass-500 visa)
headline: CoE content is genuinely good, but the ESOS/Tuition Protection framework underwriting the AUD$17,000 deposit is an orphan page no offer flow links to; and the CoE transaction is forked across a canonical path, a deprecated tree, and a LIVE published sandbox draft.
what_exists: Split across two domains at the lifecycle boundary. study.unimelb owns acceptance: /how-to-apply/{undergraduate-study|graduate-coursework-study}/international-applications/offers-and-enrolments (51-52 links each) explain offer->accept->deposit->OSHC->CoE in correct sequence; /undergraduate-study/international-applications/fees-and-payments states the AUD$17,000 deposit and links the binding Terms&Conditions PDF (2026-Tuition-Fees_Terms-and-Conditions-for-International-Students_Full_Degrees.pdf); a strong standalone /how-to-apply/conditional-confirmation-of-enrolment (35 links) fully explains conditional->full CoE conversion + eligibility. students.unimelb owns the operational CoE: /new-students/get-started/undergraduate-international/coe (Step 2, 28 links) gives the 10-working-day timeline and that CoE is required before administrative enrolment; canonical ESOS page /support-and-wellbeing/international-student-support/education-services-for-overseas-students-esos (30 links) correctly cites ESOS Act 2000 and links Tuition Protection Service (tps.gov.au), National Code 2018, the ESOS Act, and Fee Policy (MPF1325). The CoE document itself, the Acceptance & Payment Agreement, and deposit payment are all handoffs into the uncrawlable eStudent/my.unimelb portal - narrated but not transactable on the public estate.
friction: IMMIGRATION/FINANCIAL: the deposit page (where AUD$17,000 changes hands - the largest financial commitment of the journey) links the binding T&C PDF but NEVER links ESOS or the Tuition Protection Service, the statutory scheme that refunds that exact deposit if the provider defaults. A scan of every crawled links.json found ZERO content links to the ESOS page from any offer/CoE/acceptance page; the legal safety-net is described nowhere the money is committed.

IMMIGRATION: CoE is the legally-required ESOS document for the subclass-500 visa, yet it is only narrated - issuance, the Acceptance & Payment Agreement and deposit all occur inside the uncrawlable eStudent/my.unimelb portal. If the prose timeline ('within 10 working days') slips or the email is missed, there is no transactional status page and the student risks missing the visa-lodgement window for their intake.

COMPLIANCE/EQUITY: the conditional-CoE flow is hard agent-gated - 'All applications for a conditional CoE must be submitted by your authorised education agent', with a criterion 'Have appointed an authorised education agent'. A direct applicant needing a conditional CoE to make their intake must first acquire an agent, guided only by a 1-page /authorised-education-agent stub; the most time-critical CoE path is blocked for the unrepresented.

OSHC handoff is mislabeled/mismatched: the UG offer page links OSHC to /admin/student-visas/overseas-student-health-cover-oshc, but the UG fees/deposit page AND the PG offer page link the same 'OSHC' anchor to /support-and-wellbeing/international-student-support/visas/healthcare-for-other-visa-types - a page about OTHER visa types, not the subclass-500 OSHC. OSHC is a deposit-time and visa prerequisite, so a wrong destination here carries visa consequences.

No conditional->full CoE conversion page in the current-student visa tree: the only conditional-CoE explainer lives on study.unimelb (prospective). Once a student holds a conditional CoE, the canonical visa tree offers 'request a NEW CoE' but no page on meeting outstanding conditions to convert conditional->unconditional - the step their enrolment and visa depend on.
fragmentation: CoE-request exists as multiple live indexed copies of the SAME transaction: canonical /support-and-wellbeing/international-student-support/visas/apply-for-a-student-visa/request-a-new-confirmation-of-enrolment-coe AND a LIVE PUBLISHED sandbox draft /sandbox/2026-uplifts/student-visas/apply-for-a-student-visa/request-a-new-confirmation-of-enrolment-coe - plus the deprecated /student-support/ legacy tree (linked by 11 faculties) carrying a third. Three live URLs for one legally-sensitive action.

Acceptance & Payment Agreement is forked by cohort: UG uses /new-students/get-started/undergraduate-international/agreement (indexed) while the PG international offer page links /get-started/coursework-international/agreement (NOT in the page index - a redirect or dead/uncrawled path). Sibling cohorts on the same legal step land on different, inconsistently-resolving agreement URLs.

'Receive your CoE' content is itself duplicated: the Get Started Step-2 CoE page (students.unimelb, operational) and the conditional-CoE 'What is a CoE?' explainer (study.unimelb, prospective) both define the CoE and its visa role independently, with no canonical cross-link.

The legal moment straddles the prospective/current domain seam: acceptance + deposit + conditional-CoE live on study.unimelb; the ESOS framework + operational CoE + reissue live on students.unimelb - and the two trees do not reciprocally link at the offer/CoE step.
the_seam: The deposit-payment moment on study.unimelb's /fees-and-payments page. Three legally-loaded handoffs converge and none is closed: (1) OUTWARD into the eStudent/my.unimelb portal to sign the Acceptance & Payment Agreement and pay AUD$17,000 + OSHC, where the CoE is then issued entirely outside the crawlable estate; (2) the MISSING lateral link to the ESOS/Tuition Protection page on students.unimelb, so the statutory protection for that deposit is invisible exactly when the deposit is paid; (3) the MISMATCHED OSHC link routing to 'other visa types'. The student commits the largest sum of the journey, into an uncrawled portal, with the legal safety-net page orphaned and the health-cover link pointing at the wrong page.
recommendation: 1) Surface ESOS/Tuition Protection AT the commitment point: add a prominent contextual link + one-line plain-English note about the Tuition Protection Service to /fees-and-payments and every offers-and-enrolments page (the deposit is statutorily protected - say so where it is paid). 2) Make CoE a single source of truth: choose ONE canonical 'request/receive CoE' URL, 301 the deprecated /student-support/ copy and the /sandbox/2026-uplifts/ draft into it, and UNPUBLISH the sandbox (a live published draft of a visa-document transaction is a compliance hazard). 3) Fix the OSHC link target on the fees and PG offer pages to the real OSHC page, not /healthcare-for-other-visa-types. 4) Unfork the Acceptance & Payment Agreement to one working URL for UG and PG. 5) Add a current-student conditional->full CoE conversion page in the canonical visa tree, and reciprocally cross-link the study.unimelb conditional-CoE explainer with the students.unimelb Get Started CoE step. 6) Give direct (non-agent) applicants a real conditional-CoE route or de-gate it, given the visa-timing stakes.

headline: Good OSHC guidance, scattered across five competing live URLs and a slug that hides it, that falls off a transactional cliff into the uncrawlable acceptance/deposit flow — students learn the rules here but must arrange and verify cover elsewhere.
what_exists: OSHC is authored almost entirely on students.unimelb.edu.au as ONE substantive page (~8,771 chars body, 193KB) replicated across multiple live URLs. Confirmed variants of the same content: (1) CANONICAL /support-and-wellbeing/international-student-support/visas/healthcare-for-other-visa-types (fp a648a4a141966693); (2) DEPRECATED legacy /student-support/international-student-support/visas/overseas-student-health-cover-oshc (fp bfc0c2baf9883f6b, 193,333 bytes, FULLY LIVE, carries a self-correcting canonical tag to the new URL yet stays crawlable/linked); (3) SANDBOX clone /sandbox/2026-uplifts/student-visas/healthcare-for-other-visa-types (fp c8e7bb6da2eab91b, byte-identical 193,065 to canonical); (4) SANDBOX NEW-UPLIFT /sandbox/2026-uplifts/student-visas/arrange-your-overseas-student-health-cover-oshc (fp 886ca3225dfac5d3, 761,351 bytes — ~4x larger, the redesigned future version, live-published in the draft tree). A FIFTH path /admin/student-visas/overseas-student-health-cover-oshc is referenced (by study.unimelb + mdhs) but not crawled as its own page. Faculties also author their OWN OSHC pages: education.unimelb.edu.au/study/graduate-research/.../overseas-student-health-cover (live 193KB) and mdhs.unimelb.edu.au/research/research-training/.../overseas-student-health-cover (a confirmed 404 in dead-pages.csv). Content quality is genuinely strong: states OSHC is mandatory and set by Home Affairs (DHA); names the Sweden/Norway/Belgium exemptions; gives CoE-linked policy-length tables (UG/Masters, research +6 months, Study Abroad/Exchange); explains single vs couples/family/single-parent tiers; documents the Bupa 'Success' policy and Graduate OVC 10% discount; describes both arrange-paths (via University on the Student Acceptance and Payment Agreement, premium paid with the tuition deposit; or directly with another approved provider) plus extension steps; and carries the correct compliance warning that the DHA may cancel the visa if OSHC lapses. Outbound links: Bupa (UoM preferred provider), privatehealth.gov.au, and immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/student-500/length-of-stay.
friction: FINDABILITY TRAP / compliance: the canonical OSHC page lives at slug '.../visas/healthcare-for-other-visa-types' — the URL contains neither 'oshc' nor 'overseas-student-health'. A student searching the site or via SEO for 'OSHC' is more likely to land on the DEPRECATED /student-support/.../overseas-student-health-cover-oshc URL (which DOES contain the term). Immigration consequence: the discoverable URL is the legacy one the University is trying to retire.

NO IN-PAGE TRANSACTION (the cliff): you cannot arrange or verify OSHC from the guidance. The actual action — 'indicate on your Student Acceptance and Payment Agreement that you’d like the University to arrange OSHC' and pay the premium with your tuition deposit — happens inside the uncrawlable eStudent/my.unimelb acceptance flow. The page has zero deep-link into that agreement; it relies on the student remembering a checkbox at deposit time. Immigration consequence: miss the checkbox and you arrive with no cover, breaching visa condition 8501.

NO SELF-SERVE VERIFY: there is no way for a student to confirm their OSHC start/end dates cover the FULL visa length from this page. The page says dates 'will appear on your CoE' (in my.unimelb, uncrawled) and that 'other provider' policies WON'T appear on the CoE and must be supplied to DHA manually. Immigration consequence: under-length cover (OSHC ending on the course end date, not the required Nov–Dec→15 March / +2 months buffer) is a silent visa-cancellation risk the public estate cannot detect for the student.

BROKEN FACULTY ENTRY POINT: mdhs.unimelb.edu.au/research/research-training/current-students/international-students/overseas-student-health-cover returns 'Page not found' (confirmed in dead-pages.csv). MDHS graduate-research international students following their own faculty's OSHC link hit a dead end on a mandatory-compliance topic.

EXTENSION = HIGHEST-RISK MOMENT, WEAKLY WIRED: the page warns 'If your OSHC expires while you are still in Australia on a student visa, the DHA may cancel your visa,' but the remedy is to email Bupa or 'submit an enquiry with Stop 1' — again no transactional/renewal form. This is the same pattern flagged for the 'reduced study load' / 'manage enrolment changes' pages: the highest-immigration-risk action terminates in an email address, not an action.

STALE PROVIDER ARTEFACT: the linked 'Advantage' policy PDF is dated 08-02-2021 (filename token National_08022021), described as for policies 'prior to 1 March 2022'. Still surfaced inline ~4+ years later; risk a returning/transferring student reads the wrong policy terms.

RESEARCH-SCHOLARSHIP EDGE CASE under-served: the page notes scholarship OSHC may only cover single-level cover and the student must 'pay the additional amount … yourself when accepting your offer' for couples/family — another action buried in the uncrawlable acceptance flow, with no worked example or deep link.
fragmentation: FIVE+ live path variants of the SAME OSHC page: CANONICAL (support-and-wellbeing), DEPRECATED legacy (student-support, still live + linked), SANDBOX clone, SANDBOX new-uplift (761KB redesign), and an /admin/ variant referenced but uncrawled. Two are byte-identical (193,065).

INVERTED LINK EQUITY: precise anchored link-counts across the crawl show the DEPRECATED tree is linked by MORE faculties than the canonical. DEPRECATED /student-support/.../overseas-student-health-cover-oshc is linked from 7 domains / 22 pages (education, eng, fbe, mdhs, services, students, study). CANONICAL /support-and-wellbeing/.../healthcare-for-other-visa-types is linked from only 3 domains / 34 pages (students, education, study). The estate is steering students to the deprecated copy.

SLUG vs TITLE MISMATCH: every variant is titled 'Arrange your Overseas Student Health Cover (OSHC)' but the canonical slug is 'healthcare-for-other-visa-types' — the OSHC topic and the secondary 'other visa types' topic are collapsed onto one URL, so the primary mandatory-cover content is filed under a secondary-audience slug.

LIVE DRAFT IN PUBLIC: the redesigned 761KB OSHC page sits in /sandbox/2026-uplifts/ (a published draft), already internally linked (8 pages) — a future version competing with production for the same query before cutover.

DUPLICATE FACULTY AUTHORSHIP: education and mdhs each maintain a full standalone OSHC page rather than linking the hub; education's is a 193KB near-clone, mdhs's is a 404 — two more copies to keep (or fail to keep) in sync with DHA rule changes.
the_seam: The decisive handoff is into the uncrawlable eStudent/my.unimelb ACCEPTANCE + DEPOSIT flow. OSHC is not actually 'arranged' on any of these pages — it is arranged by ticking a box on the Student Acceptance and Payment Agreement and paying the premium alongside the tuition deposit, then verified by dates appearing on the CoE in my.unimelb. The public estate describes this transaction in prose but provides no deep-link into the agreement, no purchase widget, and no way to verify cover length. Secondary seams: outward to Bupa (the only provider given a real path — corporate page, app, membership-card flow) and to immi.homeaffairs.gov.au / privatehealth.gov.au for the rules. So the journey crosses three system boundaries (UoM acceptance portal, Bupa, DHA) and the estate guides the rules but not the do-it or the verify-it. Compounding the seam, the deprecated tree (forked from the wider international-support fragmentation) is the better-linked entry, so many students reach the description via a URL the University intends to retire.
recommendation: 1) COLLAPSE to one canonical OSHC page and redirect the rest. Pick a slug that contains the term (e.g. /support-and-wellbeing/international-student-support/visas/overseas-student-health-cover-oshc), split 'healthcare for other visa types' onto its own URL, 301 the deprecated /student-support/ copy and the /admin/ variant, and un-publish or gate the /sandbox/2026-uplifts/ duplicates so a live draft is not competing in search. 2) FIX THE LINK GRAPH: repoint all 7 deprecated-tree linkers (education, eng, fbe, mdhs, services, study, students) at the canonical URL; this is the same 11-faculty deprecated-link remediation already flagged for the international-support tree. 3) REPAIR the mdhs 404 (redirect to canonical) and replace education's standalone clone with a link to the hub. 4) CLOSE THE TRANSACTION CLIFF: add a real deep-link/CTA from the OSHC page into the eStudent Student Acceptance and Payment Agreement step ('Arrange OSHC now'), and a 'Check your OSHC dates' link into the my.unimelb CoE view, so arrange + verify are one click, not a prose instruction. 5) Treat OSHC-extension as a transactional moment: surface a renewal action/form, not just a Bupa email + Stop 1 enquiry, given lapse = visa cancellation. 6) Retire the 2021 'Advantage' PDF behind an accordion or date-stamp it explicitly. Severity is HIGH because every friction here has a direct visa-condition (8501 / 8105) consequence and the best-linked entry point is the copy the University is trying to deprecate.

headline: The full-time study-load visa condition is invisible at the moment international students enrol: the onboarding flow every commencing student follows never names it, and the academic enrolment task and the visa-consequence pages sit in separate trees that fail to cross-link in the dangerous direction.
what_exists: Two content systems that barely touch. (1) ONBOARDING - a dedicated international branch of /new-students: the New Student Checklist (fp 5747afaeb0a42f1c, 51 links) with an 'International students' tab, plus a 5-step 'Get Started - Undergraduate (international)' wizard: Step 2 CoE (04479254dba495ee), Step 5 'Complete your administrative enrolment' (74db2927deb2a90f), an 'agreement' page (cc35c7dd3cae04b9), and a separate under-18 path (26708f91f1061430). Step 5 is the actual enrolment task and hands off to the eStudent portal at unimelb.t1cloud.com/.../MyTasksWizard (TechnologyOne, uncrawlable). The checklist also chains to a pre-arrival sub-tree (airport-pickup-service, book-your-flights, what-to-bring-to-australia, get-set-up-in-melbourne, international-student-welcome). (2) COMPLIANCE - the study-load DEFINITION page lives in the ACADEMIC tree (/course-admin/planning-your-course-and-subjects/study-load, 10de92ba399480c8); byte-identical clone at /your-course/manage-your-course/.../study-load (both 187,717 bytes, same fingerprint) + an ?SQ_VARIATION URL = 3 live URLs for one page. It correctly states '50 credit points per half-year = standard full-time' and DOES mention visa/international/CoE. The visa-consequence pages sit in the international-support tree: 'Manage enrolment changes that affect your visa' (canonical aec4704022be7b4f, 235,692 bytes, 57 links), 'Meet your student visa and enrolment requirements' (42f4145beec8139d), and 'Reduced study load' (74073d4861a1d3c5) - which carries a REAL working Qualtrics form (q.surveys.unimelb.edu.au/jfe/form/SV_9HUpzMn4xUWLpbg, 'Apply for a Reduced Study Load'). All three of these visa pages also exist in the DEPRECATED /student-support tree and in the LIVE /sandbox/2026-uplifts draft.
friction: COMPLIANCE FAILURE - The international New Student Checklist (5747afaeb0a42f1c) NEVER mentions 'study load' or 'full-time' (verified: terms absent from main content). It lists fees, the Preventing Sexual Misconduct module, Student Charter, student card - but not the single condition that keeps a subclass-500 visa valid. The one moment 100% of arriving students pass through is silent on the rule.

COMPLIANCE FAILURE - 'Step 5: Complete your administrative enrolment' (74db2927deb2a90f), the page that launches the enrolment portal, also NEVER says 'study load', 'full-time' or 'minimum'. It tells students to 'enrol in your course' then sends them to the t1cloud eStudent wizard with no warning that they must enrol at the FULL-TIME load to satisfy their visa - the consequence is absent at the exact point of action.

COMPLIANCE FAILURE - 'Manage enrolment changes that affect your visa' (aec4704022be7b4f), the highest-immigration-risk page, does NOT link to my.unimelb/eStudent (term 'my.unimelb' absent) and does NOT link to the study-load page. It explains that dropping below full-time breaches the visa, then routes the student to immi.homeaffairs.gov.au (DHA), VEVO and Stop 1 - everywhere except the academic system where the enrolment change is actually made or undone. (Note: it DOES carry external action links + a CoE-request self-link, so the prior 'links to ZERO action forms' claim is too strong - the real defect is the missing link to the enrolment system and study-load rule.)

DEAD-END - 'Reduced study load' (74073d4861a1d3c5) has a working application form but does NOT link back to the study-load definition page, so a student mid-application cannot get back to 'what is the full-time load I'm dropping below?'

ASYMMETRIC LINKING - study-load -> reduced-study-load EXISTS, and meet-visa-conditions -> study-load EXISTS, but study-load -> manage-enrolment-changes does NOT, and manage-enrolment-changes -> study-load does NOT. The links that exist are the low-stakes ones; the link from the high-risk warning page to the place you fix the problem is the one that is missing.

EXTERNAL HANDOFF UNGUIDED - the visa decision itself crosses into immi.homeaffairs.gov.au / online.immi.gov.au (VEVO) and the enrolment task into unimelb.t1cloud.com (eStudent) - both uncrawlable. The estate names them but provides no in-context walkthrough of what to do once there, on the pages where a wrong move costs the visa.

FORKED GUIDANCE - the reduced-study-load page (198,818 bytes) and the manage-enrolment-changes page are byte-identical (or near-identical) clones across the CANONICAL /support-and-wellbeing tree AND the DEPRECATED /student-support tree, so a student can be reading a deprecated copy of life-or-death visa guidance with no signal it is the wrong one.
fragmentation: STUDY-LOAD x3: one page served at /course-admin/planning-your-course-and-subjects/study-load AND /your-course/manage-your-course/planning-your-course-and-subjects/study-load (identical fingerprint, 187,717 bytes) plus an ?SQ_VARIATION_3164566 URL - three live addresses, one truth, none owned by the international-support tree.

VISA-CONDITIONS TREE x3: 'manage enrolment changes', 'reduced study load', 'meet visa conditions' each exist in (a) CANONICAL /support-and-wellbeing/international-student-support/visas/..., (b) DEPRECATED /student-support/international-student-support/visas/visa-implications-of-changing-your-enrolment/... (byte-identical, e.g. reduced-study-load 198,818 in both), and (c) the LIVE /sandbox/2026-uplifts/student-visas/... draft (manage-enrolment-changes restructured to 803,557 bytes).

LIVE DRAFT LEAK: the /sandbox/2026-uplifts 'manage enrolment changes' draft (a418b6281a8666a2) cross-links 6 times INTO the live canonical international-support tree (Request a new CoE, extend-your-student-visa, changing-courses-or-providers, online-study, etc.), so a student or search engine landing in the published draft is bounced into production mid-journey - two parallel versions of visa-conditions content are simultaneously live and interwoven. 10 /sandbox/2026-uplifts pages were crawled.

ENROLMENT vs COMPLIANCE SPLIT BY TREE: the academic 'how to enrol / what is full-time' content sits under /course-admin and /your-course; the 'what it means for your visa' content sits under /support-and-wellbeing/international-student-support/visas; the 'do it now' onboarding sits under /new-students/get-started. Three information architectures, three owners, for one indivisible task.

DEPRECATED-TREE COMPLIANCE EXPOSURE: 11 faculties still link the deprecated /student-support/international-student-support tree (known finding), meaning faculty-referred students are routed to the legacy copies of exactly these forked visa pages.
the_seam: The academic enrolment ACTION and the visa CONDITION never meet at the point of action. A commencing international student goes /new-students checklist -> Step 5 administrative enrolment -> unimelb.t1cloud.com eStudent wizard, and at no point in that flow are the words 'full-time study load' or the subclass-500 condition shown. The study-load rule lives one tree over (under /course-admin, cloned 3x) and the consequence lives another tree over (under /visas, cloned across canonical + deprecated + a live sandbox draft). The one cross-link that would close the loop - from 'Manage enrolment changes that affect your visa' to the study-load page and to my.unimelb/eStudent where the load is set or restored - does not exist; instead that page hands off to immi.homeaffairs.gov.au and Stop 1. The seam is therefore triple: (a) onboarding-to-condition (silent), (b) condition-to-action-system (uncrawled my.unimelb/eStudent + DHA, unguided), and (c) canonical-vs-deprecated-vs-sandbox forks of the very pages that govern visa survival.
recommendation: Treat the full-time study-load condition as a first-class compliance gate embedded IN the transactional flow, not a reference page in a parallel tree. Concretely: (1) Add an unmissable 'You must enrol in a full-time study load (50 cr/half-year) to keep your student visa' callout to BOTH the international New Student Checklist (5747afaeb0a42f1c) and Step 5 administrative enrolment (74db2927deb2a90f), each linking to the study-load page and the eStudent enrolment wizard. (2) Add the missing reciprocal links: 'Manage enrolment changes that affect your visa' and 'Reduced study load' must each link to the study-load definition page AND to my.unimelb/eStudent ('change your enrolment here'); study-load must link to 'manage enrolment changes that affect your visa'. (3) Collapse the three forks - 301-redirect the deprecated /student-support/international-student-support/visas/* copies to canonical, repoint the 11 faculties, and either un-publish or noindex the /sandbox/2026-uplifts draft so search and the live tree cannot interleave it. (4) Canonicalise the 3 study-load URLs to one. (5) On 'manage enrolment changes', wrap each DHA/VEVO external handoff with one line of in-context guidance ('before you submit anything to Home Affairs, do X in eStudent / contact Stop 1') so the highest-risk external crossings are not bare links.

stage: Ongoing compliance, breach risk & support (international students) - maintaining subclass-500 visa conditions: reduced study load, unsatisfactory course progress, and enrolment changes that affect the visa.
headline: The estate's highest-consequence stage is its most broken: visa-compliance guidance is forked across THREE live, simultaneously-published trees (canonical 27 / deprecated 12 / sandbox-draft 10), 12 domains route students into the deprecated copy, and the page that warns the student visa may be cancelled gives no in-page way to lawfully action the change - the cost of the find->act break here is visa cancellation, not inconvenience.
what_exists: Three LIVE parallel visa/compliance trees on students.unimelb, all returning 200: (1) CANONICAL /support-and-wellbeing/international-student-support = 27 pages (visas hub + apply-for-a-student-visa[extend, request-a-new-CoE], meet-your-student-visa-conditions[keep-details-up-to-date, manage-enrolment-changes-that-affect-your-visa], visa-implications-of-changing-your-enrolment[reduced-study-load, changing-courses-or-providers, online-study], compassionate-or-compelling-circumstances, changing-courses + international-student-transfer-policy, ESOS, under-18 safety/supervision/fees, FAQs). (2) DEPRECATED /student-support/international-student-support = 12 pages (legacy mirror: applying-for-a-student-visa, renewing, updating-visa-status, working-on-a-student-visa, OSHC, reduced-study-load, online-study[-limitations], compassionate, under-18) - confirmed NOT redirected: absent from dead-pages.csv and crawled live in the 833-page students.unimelb set. (3) SANDBOX DRAFT /sandbox/2026-uplifts/student-visas = 10 pages, publicly indexed in freshness-signals.csv, a near-complete shadow rewrite (apply/extend/renew/request-CoE/OSHC/healthcare/meet-requirements/keep-details/manage-enrolment/support); /renew-your-student-visa/_nocache resolves to a 'University of Melbourne - Sign In' auth wall. The ESOS consumer-protection page IS substantive (National Code 2018, Tuition Protection Service, Fee Policy). Compliance triggers also surface on shared estate: course-admin/academic-progress (the unsatisfactory-progress / show-cause seam) and course-admin/leave-of-absence + course-admin/subject-withdrawal (the mechanisms that breach study-load). Evidence: analysis/full-scrape/pages-index.csv, freshness-signals.csv, dead-pages.csv; crawl/students-full/pages/<fp>/{meta,links,page}.
friction: IMMIGRATION CONSEQUENCE - find->act break on the worst page: 'manage-enrolment-changes-that-affect-your-visa' (canonical) has 57 links but only TWO transactional endpoints: VEVO (immi.homeaffairs.gov.au, an external READ-ONLY visa-detail check) and a 'Request a CMT letter' Qualtrics survey (q.surveys.unimelb.edu.au/jfe/form/SV_3OeCMVZQxczfSKi). It explicitly warns 'your student visa may be cancelled' and discusses withdrawing from your course, yet links to NO my.unimelb / eStudent path to lawfully enact the enrolment variation, leave of absence, or CoE reissue that the warning is about. The student is told they may breach their visa with no way to act.

IMMIGRATION CONSEQUENCE - the canonical risk page sends the most dangerous link to a DEPRECATED page: on manage-enrolment-changes, the 'Reduced Study Load' anchor points to /student-support/international-student-support/.../reduced-study-load (the deprecated tree), not its own canonical sibling - so following the single most visa-critical action lands the student on legacy content.

IMMIGRATION CONSEQUENCE - the canonical risk page links INTO the unfinished sandbox draft FOUR times: 'apply for a new student visa', 'Extend your student visa' (-> /renew-your-student-visa/_nocache, an auth wall), 'issued a new CoE', and request-CoE all resolve to /sandbox/2026-uplifts/... A student researching how to fix a visa-affecting change is funnelled into a draft, one branch of which dead-ends at a login screen.

IMMIGRATION CONSEQUENCE - reduced-study-load is the SAME content in THREE live places: /support-and-wellbeing/.../reduced-study-load, /support-and-wellbeing/.../visas/visa-implications-of-changing-your-enrolment/reduced-study-load, AND the deprecated /student-support/.../reduced-study-load. All three carry the identical 'Apply for a Reduced Study Load' Qualtrics form (SV_9HUpzMn4xUWLpbg). NUANCE vs brief: this page DOES expose an action form - the zero-action-form failure is specific to manage-enrolment-changes - but the form is now triplicated, so a fix or rule-change to one copy silently diverges the others.

IMMIGRATION CONSEQUENCE - unsatisfactory course progress (a subclass-500 breach risk under ESOS National Code Standard 8/9) is handled on the general course-admin/academic-progress page, linked only as a passing 'Academic progress webpage' reference from the visa pages; the international visa-breach implication of a show-cause/failure outcome is not surfaced at the academic-progress decision point itself.

Internal DUAL-PARENT fork inside the canonical tree: 'reduced-study-load' and 'changing-courses-or-providers' each exist under TWO parent lineages simultaneously (the /reduced-study-load and /changing-courses-or-providers roots AND under /visas/visa-implications-of-changing-your-enrolment/), so even within the 'correct' tree the same compliance topic has two URLs and two breadcrumb paths.

A broken/typo destination is live in the link graph: faculty/source pages link to '/student-support/international-student-support/visas/workingon-a-student-visa.' (missing hyphen, trailing dot) - a malformed work-rights URL inside the already-deprecated tree.
fragmentation: THREE live trees for one compliance domain: canonical /support-and-wellbeing/international-student-support (27 pages), deprecated /student-support/international-student-support (12), and live-published draft /sandbox/2026-uplifts/student-visas (10). The deprecated tree is NOT 301'd - it returns 200 and is a maintained parallel copy (e.g. its reduced-study-load page still carries the live Qualtrics form), so search and inbound links keep it alive.

12 DISTINCT DOMAINS link the deprecated tree (the brief said 11 faculties; it is broader), across 718 source pages. By domain: msd (614 pages), students.unimelb itself (54), study.unimelb (14), eng (11), education (7), mdhs (7), services (5), arts (4), research (3), fbe (2), law (2), mbs (1). The 11 faculty domains = arts, biomedicalsciences (via broader match), dental, education, eng, fbe, law, mbs, mdhs, medicine, msd. Dominant dead-end target: deprecated /visa-implications-of-changing-your-enrolment/advanced-standing (613 inlinks, almost all MSD).

The sandbox draft is a near-complete SHADOW of the canonical visa section (10 pages mirroring apply/extend/renew/CoE/OSHC/healthcare/meet-requirements/keep-details/manage-enrolment/support) and is publicly crawlable; canonical pages link forward into it, so it is not isolated - it is woven into the live graph.

OSHC / health-cover content is forked across all three trees: deprecated /visas/overseas-student-health-cover-oshc, canonical /visas/healthcare-for-other-visa-types, and sandbox carries BOTH /arrange-your-overseas-student-health-cover-oshc AND /healthcare-for-other-visa-types (two pages, same 'OSHC' title) - matching the brief's ~4 path-variant observation.

Net effect: a single visa-compliance question (e.g. 'can I drop a subject without breaching my visa?') has up to 3 canonical-vs-deprecated-vs-draft answers, 2 internal breadcrumb paths, and entry points scattered across 12 domains - with no machine-enforced single source of truth.
the_seam: The make-or-break seam is the handoff from KNOW (a students.unimelb visa page) to ACT (the transactional system that actually changes enrolment and triggers the visa consequence) - and on the single highest-risk page it does not exist. 'manage-enrolment-changes-that-affect-your-visa' should bridge into my.unimelb / eStudent (enrolment variation, subject withdrawal, leave-of-absence submission) and the CoE-reissue workflow; instead it bridges only to VEVO (external, read-only) and a CMT-letter survey, then hands sideways into the unfinished /sandbox draft (one branch hitting a login wall). The deeper seam is into THREE uncrawlable/external systems the public estate is supposed to guide but barely does: (a) my.unimelb / eStudent (the actual study-load and enrolment-variation transaction - no deep link from the risk page), (b) immi.homeaffairs.gov.au / VEVO (the visa itself - linked, but only to read status, not to the DHA 'change in situation' obligation), and (c) the forked internal trees themselves, where a student crossing from a faculty page is dropped into deprecated guidance. The human fallback - Stop 1 ('Submit an enquiry', 'Book a course planning appointment') - is the only consistently working path to act, which means the estate quietly relies on staff to compensate for a missing transactional bridge on its most legally-sensitive journey.
recommendation: Treat this as a compliance defect, not a content cleanup - the failure mode is visa cancellation under the ESOS National Code. Four moves: (1) Designate ONE canonical compliance tree (/support-and-wellbeing/international-student-support); 301-redirect every deprecated /student-support/international-student-support URL to its canonical equivalent and UNPUBLISH the /sandbox/2026-uplifts draft from public/crawlable space (it is a live draft with an auth-wall dead-end). (2) Repoint the 718 deprecated inlinks across the 12 domains - prioritise MSD's 614 (mostly the advanced-standing target) and fix the malformed 'workingon-a-student-visa.' anchor. (3) WIRE THE RISK PAGES TO ACTION: on 'manage-enrolment-changes-that-affect-your-visa' and the (single, de-duplicated) reduced-study-load page, add direct, prominent links to the actual transactional endpoints - my.unimelb/eStudent enrolment-variation, subject-withdrawal, leave-of-absence submission, the reduced-study-load Qualtrics form, and the CoE-reissue request - plus the DHA 'change in situation' obligation; collapse the dual-parent canonical duplicates to one URL each. (4) Surface the visa-breach consequence at the unsatisfactory-progress / academic-progress decision point for international students. Sequence: do (1) and (3) first - stopping the funnel into deprecated/draft content and giving students a way to act is what prevents an avoidable breach.

headline: The biggest international drawcard — the post-study work visa (subclass 485) — has NO destination page anywhere on the estate; it survives only as incidental "you may lose eligibility" warnings, while study-phase work rights sit on one careers page that links to neither immigration nor the visa support tree.
what_exists: Study-phase work rights: exactly ONE canonical page — students.unimelb.edu.au/careers/find-a-job/your-work-rights (35 links, 181KB, crawled 2026-06-22). It does NOT state the 8105 hour cap; it defers entirely to "your student visa conditions" and points OUT to fairwork.gov.au, ato.gov.au, studymelbourne.vic.gov.au, redcross.org.au/workrighthub. A sibling parameter-URL careers/find-a-job/?external-uuid=9df437b2... is titled "Visas for international students" but redirects into the visa support tree, not a careers page. Post-study (485): NO dedicated page exists in any of the 862 international pages. A crawl-wide search for "subclass 485 / temporary graduate / post-study work" returns only 11 pages; on inspection 9 are the SAME incidental clause ("course duration may affect your eligibility for the Temporary Graduate visa (subclass 485)... Australian Study Requirement") duplicated across the three forked support trees inside enrolment-change / online-study pages. The only substantive 485 description lives on research.unimelb.edu.au/partnerships/collaborate/engage-with-our-talent — an EMPLOYER-facing industry page ("graduates can obtain a subclass 485... live and work 1.5–4 years without employer sponsorship"), not student-facing. The canonical visas hub (/support-and-wellbeing/international-student-support/visas) has 8 children — ALL subclass-500 (apply / extend / CoE / meet-conditions / manage-enrolment-changes / keep-details / OSHC) — and ZERO for work rights or 485. Prospective estate (study.unimelb): only 1 page mentions 485 at all. Across all 1,151 careers/WIL pages in careers-pages.csv, ZERO mention 485 or post-study (grep count = 0).
friction: IMMIGRATION RISK — Work-hour cap (condition 8105) is never stated: the careers your-work-rights page only says hours are 'defined in your student visa conditions' and links to Fair Work / ATO / Study Melbourne, NOT to immi.homeaffairs.gov.au. A student cannot learn their actual fortnightly cap (the breach of which can cancel a visa) from this page — it has ZERO immi/homeaffairs links.

COMPLIANCE / RETENTION RISK — The post-study work pathway (subclass 485), the headline reason many internationals choose Australia, has no how-to-apply page, no eligibility explainer, no Australian Study Requirement guidance, and no timing/deadline guidance student-facing. The only complete 485 explanation on the whole estate is written for EMPLOYERS (research.unimelb.edu.au/.../engage-with-our-talent). Students graduate with no UoM-side guidance on securing the visa they came for.

IMMIGRATION RISK — 485 eligibility is surfaced only as a NEGATIVE warning, never as a pathway: the only student-facing 485 mentions are clauses warning that reducing study load / changing course / online-study overload 'may affect your eligibility for the Temporary Graduate visa'. A student is told what can DESTROY 485 eligibility but never how to OBTAIN it.

The careers work-rights page does NOT link to the international-student-support visa tree (0 links), and the visa tree's canonical hub does NOT contain a work-rights child. The two halves of 'work' for internationals — labour rights (careers tree) and visa work-condition (immigration tree) — are mutually unlinked islands.

manage-enrolment-changes-that-affect-your-visa is the ONLY page that links the 485 (to immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/.../temporary-graduate-485) and VEVO — but it does so as a side-effect warning of enrolment change, and several of its onward links point at the LIVE sandbox draft (/sandbox/2026-uplifts/...), not production. (This refines the brief's 'zero transactional forms' claim for this page: it carries a Qualtrics CMT-letter request form + external DHA links, though no UoM action form for 485 because none exists.)

reduced-study-load DOES carry its apply form (q.surveys.unimelb.edu.au/jfe/form/SV_9HUpzMn4xUWLpbg, 'Apply for a Reduced Study Load') across all three tree variants — so the highest-breach-risk transactional action is present here; the gap is that the consequence it warns about (losing 485 eligibility via the Australian Study Requirement) has nowhere to read more.

Graduation→post-study transition is invisible: no page connects 'you are graduating' to 'here is how/when to lodge your 485, what counts as the Australian Study Requirement, and how Careers can help you find graduate work within the 485 window.' GradWise / careers-adviser pages exist but none reference 485 or visa-bounded job search.
fragmentation: Post-study/485 content is scattered as 11 incidental fragments, 9 of which are the identical eligibility-warning clause triplicated across the three forked support trees: /support-and-wellbeing/international-student-support (canonical), /student-support/international-student-support (deprecated), and /sandbox/2026-uplifts (live published draft) — same sentence, three URLs, on each of enrolment-changes + online-study.

Work rights live in the CAREERS information architecture (careers/find-a-job/your-work-rights) while every visa/condition page lives in the SUPPORT information architecture (international-student-support/visas) — no cross-link between them, so a student researching 'can I work?' and a student researching 'will I breach my visa?' land in two separate site sections that never reference each other.

The 485's only complete description sits in a THIRD information architecture entirely — research.unimelb.edu.au industry-partnerships — addressed to employers, discoverable by students only by accident.

A deprecated /student-support/.../working-on-a-student-visa URL still resolves but the crawled body is essentially a cookie-banner shell with no substantive work-rights content, competing with the careers page for the same query.

Onward 485/visa links from the canonical manage-enrolment page point into /sandbox/2026-uplifts/student-visas/... — a live-published DRAFT tree — mixing draft and production in the single highest-immigration-risk user flow.
the_seam: The careers→immigration handoff is completely severed. The one work-rights page (careers tree) hands a student straight to external Fair Work / ATO / Study Melbourne sites with NO link to immi.homeaffairs.gov.au and NO link to the UoM visa-conditions support tree — so the public estate does not guide the crossing from 'labour rights' into 'visa work-condition (8105).' The mirror seam is graduation→post-study: at the moment a student needs to lodge a subclass 485 (an immigration.gov.au/Home Affairs transaction the estate never owns), the only UoM touchpoint is an enrolment-change page that links the 485 as a warning, routed partly through the live /sandbox draft tree. Careers/GradWise (which should own the 485-bounded graduate job search) and the visa tree (which should own the 485 application) never connect — the drawcard falls into the gap between two information architectures and an uncrawled external system.
recommendation: Create a single canonical "Working in Australia: during study and after you graduate" hub as a child of the canonical visas hub (/support-and-wellbeing/international-student-support/visas), and bidirectionally link it with careers/find-a-job/your-work-rights. It must: (1) STATE the condition 8105 fortnightly work-hour cap explicitly (not defer to 'your visa conditions') and link to the immi.homeaffairs.gov.au student-500 conditions page; (2) add a dedicated, student-facing subclass 485 page — eligibility, the Australian Study Requirement, when/how to lodge, the post-study work-window — replacing the scattered warning-only clauses and linking to immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/.../temporary-graduate-485 and VEVO; (3) connect graduation→post-study by linking GradWise / careers-adviser to the 485 page so graduate job-search is framed within the 485 window; (4) surface the 485 as a drawcard on study.unimelb (currently 1 page) at the recruitment stage. Cleanup: collapse the deprecated /student-support tree and the /sandbox/2026-uplifts draft so the 485/visa flow points only at production; de-duplicate the triplicated eligibility clause to the new hub. Quick win: add the missing immi.homeaffairs.gov.au + visa-tree links to the existing careers your-work-rights page today.
